roles.name: Kannushi of the High Shrine
roles.description: Chief priest of the design — keeper of the over-arching solution and its sacred lines.

roles.name: Daimyō of Delivery
roles.description: Lord of the project domain — marshals retainers, holds the timeline, and answers to the Emperor.

roles.name: Keeper of the Torii
roles.description: Guardian of the gate — tends fields, layouts, and permission sets between the sacred and profane.

roles.name: Swordsmith of Sagami
roles.description: Forges Apex and LWC blades, folded a thousand times in the fires of the platform.

roles.name: Shōgun of the Western Shore
roles.description: Supreme commander of Salesforce craft in the Land of the Setting Sun — sets standards and guides the senior smiths.

roles.name: Senior Onmyōji
roles.description: Master diviner of customer requirements — reads the stars and the data with practiced art.

roles.name: Apprentice Onmyōji
roles.description: Carries divination scrolls and configuration across the realm under the senior diviner's eye.

roles.name: Bridge-Master of Uji
roles.description: Architect of the great spans between Salesforce and the realms beyond — APIs, events, callouts.

roles.name: Senior Bridge-Builder of Uji
roles.description: Lays the cypress beams of middleware and message flow under the Bridge-Master's plan.

roles.name: Apprentice Bridge-Builder
roles.description: Learns the craft of integration — webhooks, batch, and the careful patterns of error handling.

roles.name: Tea Master of Quality
roles.description: Tends the cha-no-yu of the build — finds the bitterness in the brew before it reaches the Emperor.

roles.name: Herald of the Imperial Court
roles.description: Lifts the Imperial banner for product and message — leads all marketing craft.

roles.name: Senior Court Poet
roles.description: Composes waka and campaign strategy — sounds the call across the provinces.

roles.name: Court Poet
roles.description: Carries the campaign forward into the field — content, creative, and the daily ink.

roles.name: Council Scribe of Heian-kyō
roles.description: Records what is needed and what is promised — translates between mortal Customer and the courtly craft of design.

roles.name: Master Swordsmith of Yamato
roles.description: Elder smith of the western forge — reviews every blade before it leaves the anvil.

roles.name: Junior Onmyōji
roles.description: Newest student of divination — first year on the path of the platform.

_realmNotes: windowOffsetWeeks values are negative so every realm starts at least one month in the past — past weeks get Time Entries (Step 6), future weeks get Schedules. Workstreams are organised as parent scope items (each with 2-3 child sub-scopes) — the wizard inserts parents first, then children with Parent_WorkStream__c set.

realms.projectName: Hyakki Yagyō — Night Parade Surveillance Build

realms.subAccountName: Guild of the Seven Lucky Gods

kami-of-takamagahara.name: Open the Heavenly Cave
kami-of-takamagahara.description: Discovery sign-off — Amaterasu emerges from Amanoiwato and light returns to the project.

kami-of-takamagahara.name: Forge Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi
kami-of-takamagahara.description: Build kickoff: the grass-cutting sword pulled from Yamata-no-Orochi's tail.

kami-of-takamagahara.name: Audience at the High Plain
kami-of-takamagahara.description: Mid-project gate: review with the council of the eight million kami.

kami-of-takamagahara.name: Tribute to Susanoo
kami-of-takamagahara.description: Phase invoice trigger: offerings sent to the Storm God's hall.

kami-of-takamagahara.name: Descent of Ninigi
kami-of-takamagahara.description: Go-Live: the heavenly grandson descends to rule the islands.

kami-of-takamagahara.name: Shrine Hand-off
kami-of-takamagahara.description: Hyper-care close: shrine stewardship passed to the kannushi.

yokai-and-bakemono.name: Lantern Lighting
yokai-and-bakemono.description: Project kickoff: paper lanterns lit along the mountain path.

yokai-and-bakemono.name: Hyakki Yagyō Procession
yokai-and-bakemono.description: Build phase: the Night Parade of One Hundred Demons takes the road.

yokai-and-bakemono.name: Crossroads Trial
yokai-and-bakemono.description: UAT gate: the road of the crossroads holds against mortal travelers.

yokai-and-bakemono.name: Charm-ward Activation
yokai-and-bakemono.description: Go-Live: ofuda hung at every gate.

yokai-and-bakemono.name: Return to the Mountain
yokai-and-bakemono.description: Hyper-care close: the yokai retreat to their wild places.

yokai-and-bakemono.name: Tribute of Cucumbers
yokai-and-bakemono.description: Final invoice: kappa tribute paid in cucumbers and sake.

imperial-line-and-heroes.name: Imperial Audience
imperial-line-and-heroes.description: Discovery: presented to the Chrysanthemum Throne.

imperial-line-and-heroes.name: Forge the Imperial Regalia
imperial-line-and-heroes.description: Build: Mirror, Sword, and Jewel readied for the new Emperor.

imperial-line-and-heroes.name: Shōgun's Audit
imperial-line-and-heroes.description: Solution review with the military commander himself.

imperial-line-and-heroes.name: Battle of Dan-no-Ura
imperial-line-and-heroes.description: Go-Live: decisive engagement at the western straits.

imperial-line-and-heroes.name: Court Returns to Kyōto
imperial-line-and-heroes.description: Project close: the Imperial Court returns to the capital.

imperial-line-and-heroes.name: Final Tribute
imperial-line-and-heroes.description: Final invoice from the Imperial Treasury.

seven-lucky-gods-and-folk-deities.name: Council on the Treasure Ship
seven-lucky-gods-and-folk-deities.description: Kickoff council aboard the Takarabune at New Year's tide.

seven-lucky-gods-and-folk-deities.name: Cast the Lucky Net
seven-lucky-gods-and-folk-deities.description: Discovery: Ebisu casts his net for the first reports.

seven-lucky-gods-and-folk-deities.name: Strike the Mallet of Plenty
seven-lucky-gods-and-folk-deities.description: Build: Daikoku swings the mallet — dashboards stamped from gold.

seven-lucky-gods-and-folk-deities.name: Gift-Giving at the Pier
seven-lucky-gods-and-folk-deities.description: UAT acceptance: gifts of the Seven distributed to each crew member.

seven-lucky-gods-and-folk-deities.name: Sailing the Treasure Home
seven-lucky-gods-and-folk-deities.description: Go-Live + handoff: the Takarabune docks at the harbor of fortune.

seven-lucky-gods-and-folk-deities.name: Final Recompense
seven-lucky-gods-and-folk-deities.description: Final invoice from the Guild of the Seven Lucky Gods.

Amaterasu.notes: Retreat into the Amanoiwato cave — the world goes dark.

Susanoo.notes: Banished to Izumo to slay Yamata-no-Orochi — pre-arranged.

Tsukuyomi.notes: Night-shift moon duty — never speaks to his sister anyway.

Inari.notes: Rice harvest blessing tour — quarterly visit to all 30,000 shrines.

Kitsune.notes: Nine-tail molting season — cannot be seen in current state.

Tengu.notes: Mountain ascetic training with yamabushi at Mount Kurama.

Yuki-onna.notes: Off-season — melts above 5°C, returns when snow falls again.

Tanuki.notes: Sake brewery festival — purely for inventory inspection purposes.

Yamato Takeru.notes: Eastern campaign — pacifying the Emishi tribes.

Tomoe Gozen.notes: Naginata championship at the Genpei tournament.

Benkei.notes: Standing watch on Gojō Bridge — refuses to sit down even off-duty.

Momotaro.notes: Onigashima expedition with the dog, monkey, and pheasant.

Hotei.notes: Sack restocking — gifts for all the children of the village.

Benzaiten.notes: Biwa recital at Enoshima Shrine — annual.
